diff_hunk:
@@ -120,12 +120,12 @@
 - [x] **Add HTTP security response headers (OWASP / W3C)**  
   Missing: `X-Frame-Options`, `X-Content-Type-Options` (only set on file downloads today), `Content-Security-Policy`, `Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy`. Consider `NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders` or `NWebsec` and add as middleware in the pipeline.
 
-- [ ] **Implement ETag / Conditional Requests (RFC 7232)**  
+- [ ] **Implement ETag / Conditional Requests (RFC 7232)**  - No needed
body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Minor typo in the added text.

```suggestion
- [ ] **Implement ETag / Conditional Requests (RFC 7232)**  - Not needed
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

According to the repository style guide (line 182), explicit typing should be used for complex inferred types. `MapGroup` returns a `RouteGroupBuilder`, which is not a trivial type like a literal or a `new()` expression.

```c#
        RouteGroupBuilder wolverineGroup = app.MapGroup("");
```

<details>
<summary>References</summary>

1. Declare full types unless the RHS is trivially obvious (e.g., new(), literals). Avoid var for complex inferred types. <sup>([link](https://github.com/zribktad/API-Template-Modules/blob/main/.gemini/styleguide.md))</sup>
</details>

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The use of `var` here violates the repository style guide for complex inferred types. Also, the namespace prefix `Microsoft.AspNetCore.WebUtilities.` is redundant as the namespace is already imported.

```c#
        Dictionary<string, Microsoft.Extensions.Primitives.StringValues> parsedQuery = QueryHelpers.ParseQuery(
            request.QueryString.Value
        );
```

This test uses `?limit=10`, but the codebase’s pagination input is `PageSize`/`pageSize` (see `SharedKernel.Application.DTOs.PaginationFilter`). Using a non-standard query param here reduces the test’s value; update it to `?pageSize=10` (and adjust the inline comment) so the generated links reflect real request shapes.
The inline comment says "limit 10", but the codebase uses `PageSize` terminology throughout. Consider updating the comment to avoid confusion when reading these tests.

diff_hunk:
@@ -120,12 +120,12 @@
 - [x] **Add HTTP security response headers (OWASP / W3C)**  
   Missing: `X-Frame-Options`, `X-Content-Type-Options` (only set on file downloads today), `Content-Security-Policy`, `Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy`. Consider `NetEscapades.AspNetCore.SecurityHeaders` or `NWebsec` and add as middleware in the pipeline.
 
-- [ ] **Implement ETag / Conditional Requests (RFC 7232)**  
+- [ ] **Implement ETag / Conditional Requests (RFC 7232)**  - No needed
body: Grammar/wording: "No needed" should be "Not needed" (or remove the note entirely).

`CreateLink` only forces `pageNumber` into the generated URL. If the request omitted `pageSize` (common when callers rely on defaults) or if the server clamps the requested size, the `next/prev/first/last` URLs may not reflect the actual `PageSize` used to produce this response. Consider also setting `pageSize` from `pagedResponse.PageSize` when generating the link URLs (while still preserving other query params).
`SharedKernel.Domain.Common` is imported but not used in this file. With TreatWarningsAsErrors enabled, this will fail the build; remove the unused using.
`Microsoft.Extensions.Primitives` is imported but not used in this test file. With TreatWarningsAsErrors enabled, the test project build will fail; remove the unused using.

`WolverineFx.Http` is added as a dependency here, but there are no references to types/namespaces from that package in `src/SharedKernel` (searching the project shows no `WolverineFx.Http`/`Wolverine.Http` usages). If it’s not required for compilation/runtime, consider removing it to avoid an unnecessary transitive dependency.

`Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.Extensions` is imported but not used in this file. With TreatWarningsAsErrors enabled, this will fail the build; remove the unused using (or use the extension methods if intended).

`AppendLinkHeader` builds both the RFC 8288 `Link` header and multiple `X-Pagination-*` headers. The method name is misleading and makes call sites harder to understand; consider renaming to something like `AppendPaginationHeaders` (or splitting link vs pagination metadata).

The `last` link uses `1` when `TotalPages` is `0`, but `X-Pagination-Total-Pages` is still set to `0`. This can produce inconsistent pagination metadata (e.g., `last` points to page 1 while headers report 0 pages). Consider aligning the conventions: either omit first/last links when `TotalPages == 0`, or clamp the exposed total pages consistently when generating both the links and `X-Pagination-Total-Pages`.

Headers are added using `response.Headers.Append(...)`. If this helper is invoked more than once in a request (e.g., filter registered twice, nested pipeline, etc.), it will duplicate `Link` / `Access-Control-Expose-Headers` / `X-Pagination-*` values. Consider making this idempotent by assigning via the indexer (replace) and, for `Access-Control-Expose-Headers`, merging with any existing exposed headers instead of blindly appending.
